items.headline: Chicago Sky guard Rachel Banham engaged to fellow Minnesota star Andre Hollins
items.summary: Rachel Banham became engaged to Andre Hollins on January 2-3, 2022. The proposal took place at Williams Arena in a surprise from her longtime boyfriend, a former Gophers basketball standout. Both are considered among the best point guards in Minnesota Gophers history.

items.headline: Kysre Gondrezick and Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown split after public romance
items.summary: WNBA star Kysre Gondrezick and NBA superstar Jaylen Brown officially split in 2025 after months of public dating. The two were spotted together at the Celtics' 2024 NBA Championship parade and at the ESPYs where Brown introduced her as 'my date'. Their relationship drew significant media attention before the breakup.

items.headline: Golden State's Temi Fagbenle pursues acting dream alongside WNBA career
items.summary: Center Temi Fagbenle, sister to actor O-T Fagbenle and born to a Nigerian family in Baltimore, has always held acting as one of her first loves. Theater and film performance run deep in her family lineage. Fagbenle credits watching her brothers grow up in the theater and seeing the impact of their performances as inspiring her own artistic aspirations beyond basketball.

items.title: Playoff Officiating Advocacy & $15K Fine
items.summary: Fined $15,000 and suspended one game after blasting WNBA officiating during 2025 playoffs, calling the league's referee assignment 'malpractice.' Known outspoken advocate for players' concerns on officiating and working conditions.
items.headline: Playoff Officiating Advocacy & $15K Fine

items.title: First Asian American Head Coach in WNBA History
items.summary: Hired as first head coach of Golden State Valkyries (Oct 2024), becoming first Asian American head coach in WNBA history. Third-generation Japanese-American; first Asian American to play in NWBL. Previously first female head coach in Japan's top pro men's league.
items.headline: First Asian American Head Coach in WNBA History

items.title: Caitlin Clark's Head Coach: Managing Gen Z's Biggest Sports Star
items.summary: Hired as Indiana Fever head coach (Nov 2024) to lead WNBA Rookie of the Year Caitlin Clark. White previously called many of Clark's college games. Clark immediately responded to hire: 'Fevvvv shooowww' on Instagram. High-profile coaching dynamics with Gen Z's biggest basketball talent.
items.headline: Caitlin Clark's Head Coach: Managing Gen Z's Biggest Sports Star

items.title: Return to Indiana: College Legend Turns WNBA Coach
items.summary: Returns to Indiana after two seasons with Connecticut Sun. Previously Fever head coach (2015-2016). Indiana basketball legend recognized for strong coaching acumen and ability to manage star talent. Second stint designed to maximize Fever's contention window.
items.headline: Return to Indiana: College Legend Turns WNBA Coach

items.title: Legacy Succession: Top Assistant Chelsea Lyles Named FGCU Coach
items.summary: When Smesko left for Atlanta, his longtime top assistant Chelsea Lyles was named FGCU's next head coach, continuing dynasty. Represents deep bench-building and mentorship culture in Smesko's program.
items.headline: Legacy Succession: Top Assistant Chelsea Lyles Named FGCU Coach

items.title: NBA Veteran Coach: 13 Years with Golden State, Portland, Orlando
items.summary: Lengthy NBA assistant coaching resume: Cleveland Cavaliers (2011-13), Portland Trail Blazers (2013-2021, including associate head coach 2019-21), Orlando Magic (2021-23). Brought proven NBA systems and experience to WNBA.
items.headline: NBA Veteran Coach: 13 Years with Golden State, Portland, Orlando

items.title: Chicago Sky's Committed Coach Through Rebuild
items.summary: Named Chicago Sky head coach (Nov 2024). Sky committed to Marsh for at least 2026 season despite rough first year. Represents investment in stability and long-term vision over reactionary coaching carousel.
items.headline: Chicago Sky's Committed Coach Through Rebuild

items.title: Dallas Wings GM Curt Miller's Replacement: Restoring Stability
items.summary: Named Dallas Wings head coach (Oct 2025). Replaces Chris Koclanes (10-34, one season). Fernandez brings 25 years of college success and stability after Wings chaos. Age 53, deep experience.
items.headline: Dallas Wings GM Curt Miller's Replacement: Restoring Stability

items.title: Finalized Coaching Staff Including Mike Neighbors (Arkansas Dynasty Coach)
items.summary: Finalized 2026 coaching staff with former Arkansas head coach Mike Neighbors as assistant. Also hired Camille Smith, David Adkins, Empress Davenport. Established, proven coaching infrastructure.
items.headline: Finalized Coaching Staff Including Mike Neighbors (Arkansas Dynasty Coach)

items.title: Connecticut Sun's 7th Head Coach: International Hiring Strategy
items.summary: Named Connecticut Sun head coach (Dec 2024). Native of Clermont-Ferrand, France. Represents Sun's international hiring strategy to elevate program with European success credentials. First Sun coach from outside North American traditional pipeline.
items.headline: Connecticut Sun's 7th Head Coach: International Hiring Strategy

items.title: 22+ Years College Head Coach Experience
items.summary: 22+ years as collegiate head coach. Leaves stable, successful Utah program for Sparks rebuild. Brings extensive leadership experience and NCAA tournament success to WNBA.
items.headline: 22+ Years College Head Coach Experience
Skills challenge prize funds home with girlfriend Isabelle Harrison
items.summary: Natasha Cloud won the 2025 WNBA All-Star Skills Challenge for $55,000 and publicly announced she would use the prize money as a down payment on a house with her partner, Isabelle Harrison, a New York Liberty teammate. Cloud described Harrison as a 'beautiful straight woman that fell in love with me.' The couple now lives together in Brooklyn.

items.headline: Acquires Turkish citizenship, changes name to Beren officially
items.summary: In March 2026, Kennedy Burke officially acquired Turkish citizenship and adopted the given name Beren. She began playing for the Turkey women's national basketball team in the 2026 FIBA Women's Basketball World Cup Qualifying Tournaments while maintaining her WNBA role with New York Liberty.

items.headline: Gives birth to second child mid-season, returns for 2025 playoffs
items.summary: Cheyenne Parker-Tyus gave birth to son Yoshua on July 1, 2025, while signed with Las Vegas. She announced her return on Instagram July 15 and made her comeback September 9, scoring 8 points in 8 minutes. The Aces went on to win the 2025 WNBA Championship with her on the roster.

items.headline: Selected by Golden State Valkyries in WNBA expansion draft, leaves Aces
items.summary: In December 2024, Kate Martin was selected by the Golden State Valkyries in the WNBA expansion draft from Las Vegas. The Iowa standout had been a fan-favorite rookie with the Aces, with her jersey being one of the best-selling in the league before the move to San Francisco.

items.headline: Removes girlfriend Claire Gransee photos after college relationship
items.summary: Kate Martin dated Claire Gransee, whom she met at University of Iowa, publicly in 2024. The couple went public in June 2024 but have since removed photos together from social media, suggesting their relationship status may have changed.

items.headline: Unrivaled 3-on-3 league boosted confidence ahead of 2026 season
items.summary: Malonga credited her offseason participation in the Unrivaled 3-on-3 league for building confidence in high-pace basketball. Playing at the highest pace she's ever experienced, Malonga said she didn't need to change how she loves to play—scoring, going hard, and grabbing double-doubles.

items.headline: Married to Devon Brewer, former Arizona defensive back who proposed 2020
items.summary: Aari McDonald is married to Devon Brewer, a former University of Arizona defensive back who proposed after the Wildcats' Pac-12 Tournament elimination in 2020. Her brother Tre'von Willis played basketball at UNLV.

items.headline: Mystics rookie makes All-Star game in historic 2025 season
items.summary: Sonia Citron, the third overall draft pick in 2025, became just the second time in WNBA history that rookie teammates were selected as All-Stars, alongside Kiki Iriafen. She set a franchise single-season scoring record with 657 points and averaged 14.9 points, 4.0 rebounds, and 2.4 assists per game.

items.headline: Citron becomes Mystics' first rookie with 25 pts and 10 rebounds
items.summary: On June 22, 2025, in an overtime game against Dallas Wings, Sonia Citron scored 27 points and grabbed 11 rebounds, making her the first Washington Mystics rookie to record 25-plus points and 10-plus rebounds in a single game.

items.headline: Nelson-Ododa launches Kayelise wellness brand for women's health
items.summary: Connecticut Sun star Olivia Nelson-Ododa founded Kayelise, a wellness brand honoring her grandmother Herenia Ododa who died of cervical cancer. The brand debuted at Paris Fashion Week in March 2025 with the teal silk 'Herenia Robe' and advocates for ovarian and cervical cancer awareness.

items.headline: Morrow joins elite rookie class with 300 pts, 280 rebs as freshman
items.summary: Connecticut Sun #7 pick Aneesah Morrow became just the second rookie in franchise history to finish with 300+ points and 280+ rebounds in a single season, joining former Rookie of the Year Tina Charles. She earned a starting role down the stretch.

items.headline: Burrell participates in inaugural Unrivaled 3-on-3 league
items.summary: LA Sparks guard Rae Burrell appeared and played in the inaugural 2025 season of Unrivaled, the women's 3-on-3 basketball league founded by Napheesa Collier and Breanna Stewart.
